title: Break the placement lattice: rotated per-level grids + full-cell jitter
description: Axis-aligned level grids with ±22% jitter put strokes in horizontal rows whose periodic overlap reads as fine corduroy/scanline ripple across flat regions at small Size (cat-render review). Fix: each level's candidate grid rotates by its own angle (seed/grid-phi, mirrored via u_cphi/u_sphi + rotated-bbox enumeration + out-of-image discard) and jitter widens to the full cell (±sp/2).
close_reason: Fixed + verified: coyote at Size 6 / Detail 1.0 / 120k (the corduroy settings) renders with no ripple or row structure; GPU/CPU exact (23993=23993 incl Σalpha — float rotation kept count parity); 48 tests + check green; golden re-pinned (560).

title: Artifact cleanup: halos, edge-echo worms, flat-area streaks
description: Three artifact families from the cat-render review: (1) halos around silhouettes — box heavy-blur bleeds across boundaries; edge-preserving mix (fall back to light blur where |heavy-raw| ramps 0.06..0.16) fixes; (2) wormy echo lines offset from edges — E fused at tensor-blur width seeds fine strokes in a band; E² in the sharp map concentrates to the edge core; (3) streaks in flat backgrounds — broad strokes keep only 40% tone jitter.
close_reason: Fixed + verified: coyote silhouette has no halo ring, street has no edge-echo worms, flat walls smooth; lcavatar background clean. GPU/CPU exact (24119=24119 incl Σalpha), 48 tests + check green, golden re-pinned (558). Also diagnosed jolt AOT-cache staleness (size-keyed invalidation ate a same-length golden re-pin; recorded via bd remember).

title: Smooth stroke transitions away from edges + sixth detail level
description: Banding fix: broad strokes (base+lvl1) colour from a HEAVY blur (scale-matched smoothing, radius h/80) and the blur->raw blend leans blur (t=0.15+0.85*max(coh,D), was 0.55+0.45) — flat regions paint with the smooth field (seamless gradients), edges stay raw. Sixth placement level (size/32, 2 segs, 0.5 steps, 10% bend) for the really small marks; Detail scale now 1+round(detail*5) max 6.
close_reason: Done + verified: lcavatar/coyote backgrounds render as smooth gradients (banding gone), edges keep traced detail; 6-level pyramid GPU/CPU exact (99847=99847 incl Σalpha — the constant-bound decode loop holds for slot 5); 48 tests + check green; golden + splat-record-spec re-pinned for the blur-leaning blend.

title: More small detail: 120k budget, 5th placement level, sharper fine marks
description: Raise splat ceiling (quad renderer removed the pixels-x-splats wall): budget 48k->120k, max-splats 131072, Splats slider to 120k, default 24k. Add a 5th placement level (ssz=size/16, 2-segment marks, 0.6 steps, 15% bend, sharp-map placement); Detail 1.0 now reaches it. GA_PAINTER_DETAIL headless override.
close_reason: Done + verified: coyote at 100k/detail-1.0 has real eyes with pupils, legible TOCK, fur direction; street has near-readable sign numbers. GPU/CPU exact at 90310=90310 and 30210=30210. Includes the gap-bug fix: Apple GLSL mis-executed the uniform-bounded level-decode loop's last slot in the GS (base level emitted nothing at 5 levels -> truncated underpainting = gaps); constant-bound loop fixes it. 48 tests + check green.

title: Scale-relative stroke parameters + per-scale detail maps
description: Coarse-to-fine pass adjusts its behaviour to the scale it paints at: per-level segment count (6/4/3), step length (1.1/0.9/0.75·ssz), curvature share (1.0/0.55/0.3); finest levels threshold + derive D from a sharp fine-band Haar map (minimal smoothing) instead of the smoothed aggregate.
close_reason: Implemented + verified: layer-params carries per-level segs/stepf/bendf/sharp?; stroke-segments + GS chain loop consume them; wavelet/placement-map returns :sharp (fine-band Haar, minimal smoothing, same normalize+fuse) uploaded as detail-tex .g; budget accounts per-level segs with √segs spacing. GPU/CPU exact (27450=27450 incl Σalpha); 48 tests + check green; street figures/signs and coyote face/TOCK recovered while stroke look holds.

title: Brush-stroke detail: traced tapered segment chains along edge flow
description: Fine-level seeds trace a streamline along the orientation field (K~6 steps, Perlin bend via Curvature), emitting small gaussian segments with size+alpha taper toward the tail — brush lines fading out at the end, layered over the base underpainting. Base layer (low-wavelet-detail fill) unchanged. Per-splat alpha → 3-texel record (12 floats) through gen GS (o_a/o_b/o_c), pack-splats, all render shaders, preview; budget accounts K segments per fine seed.
close_reason: Fine-level detail is now painted as brush strokes: stroke-segments (CPU) / GS chain loop (GPU) trace 6 segments along the edge-tangent orientation field, sign-continuous, Perlin-bent by Curvature, with width (−45% t^1.5) and alpha (−65% t²) tapering to the tail. Per-splat alpha rides a 3-texel record through pack-splats, all render shaders, preview. Fine seeds √6 sparser so the budget scale (stroke width) matches the old dabs — strokes organize the same paint along edges instead of fattening it. Verified: GPU/CPU exact (14083=14083 incl Σalpha), 48 tests + check green, 48k stress clean; lcavatar/coyote/street read as layered paintings.

title: Bilinear cos2θ/sin2θ orientation sampling — kill the edge zipper
description: Fine splats sample theta nearest-neighbor from the 384px tensor grid → stair-stepped orientations along every contour → regular sawtooth (lcavatar). Store cos(2θ)/sin(2θ)+coherence in the noise fields, matched manual bilinear on CPU (sample-fields) and GPU (gen GS); per-stroke size/tone jitter becomes per-seed hash01 (not a spatial field).
close_reason: Zipper eliminated: prep-noise stores cos2θ/sin2θ+coherence, sample-fields + gen GS both do matched manual bilinear in double-angle space; per-seed size/tone jitter is hash01 (not a Perlin field). Verified: lcavatar edges smooth, GPU/CPU exact count match (14022=14022), 47 tests green.

title: GPU splat generation
description: Move per-render splat-field generation off the CPU (~800ms at 40k splats) onto the GPU via an FBO render-to-texture pass. macOS OpenGL 4.1: no compute shaders/SSBOs, so use render-to-texture. CPU splat-field stays the golden-tested reference; the GLSL generation shader mirrors splat-record + layered-means, verified structurally (check.clj) and visually (compare GPU render to the CPU preview).
acceptance_criteria: High-detail renders (Splats 40k) generate on the GPU; output visually matches the CPU preview; all existing tests + golden stay green.
close_reason: GPU splat generation done. ~50x faster (3ms vs ~150ms at 14k; 3.2ms at 48k). GPU is now the default path (GA_PAINTER_CPU_GEN falls back to the tested CPU reference). Bottleneck moved to the O(pixels×splats) render (separate bead).

title: Palette on the GPU path + meaningful Opacity
description: Palette slider is dead on the default GPU path (quantization only in CPU splat-field). Build palette from image pixels (palette/build-palette) at render time, upload as uniform vec3 array (≤64), snap colors in the gen GS; CPU path uses the same image-derived palette for parity. Opacity: with per-splat alpha it becomes stroke translucency/glazing — document + verify visible effect.
close_reason: Controls removed instead of fixed per user decision: Palette slider + quantize branch + palette.clj deleted; Opacity slider removed (opacity stays an internal constant 0.9 — per-splat alpha from the brush-stroke work supersedes it as the visible translucency mechanism).

title: Render is O(pixels × splats) brute force — GPU hang at ~48k splats
description: The fragment shader loops over EVERY splat per pixel (splat-painter.shader fs-src / fs-src-buf). At the Splats slider max (48k) over a full-res framebuffer this is ~3.4e10 iterations/frame and macOS's GPU watchdog kills it (GLDRendererMetal 'Caused GPU Hang Error'). Pre-existing: affects both the CPU-gen (tiled sampler2D) and GPU-gen (samplerBuffer) render paths — same shader. Safe up to ~38k; hangs at 48k. Now the dominant cost since GPU generation made field-building ~50x faster (3ms). Fix = spatial acceleration: bin splats into screen tiles (or a coarse grid) so each pixel only tests nearby splats, or cap the effective render count. Out of scope for f10 (generation).
design: Replace the per-pixel all-splats loop with per-splat quad rasterization + hardware blending (the standard 3DGS approach): draw n*6 vertices attribute-less (gl_VertexID -> splat index + corner; attribute-less draws already proven working here by the TF generation pass), VS fetches the splat from the samplerBuffer, emits a 3.5-sigma axis-aligned quad (half-extents 3.5*sqrt(c00), 3.5*sqrt(c11) = exact marginal stdevs), FS evaluates only that splat's gaussian, premultiplied over-blend back-to-front (reverse index order = coarse-to-fine, buffer is finest-first) onto a bg-cleared, scissored image rect. Mathematically identical compositing; work drops from pixels*splats (3.4e10) to sum of quad areas (~2-3e8). Keep the loop renderer behind GA_PAINTER_LOOP_RENDER for A/B.
close_reason: Replaced the O(pixels×splats) fragment loop with per-splat quad rasterization + hardware blending: attribute-less draw of 6·n vertices, VS fetches the splat from the samplerBuffer via gl_VertexID, emits a 3.5σ quad (marginal-stdev AABB), FS evaluates only that gaussian, premultiplied over-blend back-to-front (reverse index = coarse-to-fine, buffer is finest-first), scissored to the image rect. A/B vs the loop renderer at 14k: visually identical. 48k coyote (the hang case): renders clean, no watchdog errors. Loop renderer kept behind GA_PAINTER_LOOP_RENDER; CPU-gen fallback path unchanged.

title: Investigate detail preservation, esp. dark regions
description: Rendering still doesn't look fully natural and DARK sections lose a lot of detail. Review how the wavelet detail map and the structure-tensor edge detection are used together (detail-fraction thresholds, deff saturation, per-level thresholds, colour blur->raw blend) and whether detail is preserved in low-luma regions. The wavelet detail is likely luma-magnitude biased so dark areas read as low-detail and get few fine strokes; consider luma-normalized or contrast-relative detail, and check the blur base isn't washing out dark detail.
design: Root cause (measured): Haar detail + tensor gradients are absolute-luma; coyote.jpg dark cells (85% of frame, luma<0.25) pass lvl-1 threshold at 9.6% vs 81% for bright cells. Fix confined to analysis maps (zero GLSL: maps are CPU-computed per load + uploaded as textures, GPU consumes automatically): new wavelet/placement-map = gamma-corrected luma (^1/2.2) into Haar + tensor, local-contrast normalization fused with global via max(), edge-strength (sqrt(grad2/gmax)) fusion so contours get fine splats, finer res + smaller blur. Colour crispness free via existing t=0.55+0.45*max(coh,D). Implementation delegated to dirge; review + GPU-verify + re-pin golden after.
acceptance_criteria: Dark, detailed regions (e.g. shadowed shopfronts) keep their fine strokes; output reads more natural. Field golden updated if the change is intentional.
close_reason: Placement map is now luma-relative + edge-fused (wavelet/placement-map): gamma-corrected (1/2.2) luma into Haar + structure tensor, local-contrast normalization fused with global via max(), tensor edge strength (sqrt(grad2/gmax), 0.85 weight) so contours get fine splats, 512 max-side + /40 blur. Measured on coyote.jpg dark cells: frac>=lvl1 0.10 -> 0.82, frac>=lvl2 0.009 -> 0.22. Verified: coyote face/fur + gravestone text now render, street/watch improved with no regression, GPU/CPU exact-match holds (14111=14111), 57 tests + check green. Implemented by dirge from spec, reviewed + verified here.

title: Verify GPU generation vs CPU golden
description: Per the chosen approach (CPU stays the tested reference): add check.clj structural asserts for the generation shader (uniforms + splat-record/placement math present), and visually compare a GPU render to the CPU preview for the same input. Confirm the field golden + splat-record spec stay green.
close_reason: GPU generation via transform feedback: gen.clj VS+GS mirrors seed/splat-record + layer-params + noise; upload-fields! uploads detail/noise/blur/raw/perm textures; TF+geometry-shader compaction captures survivors into a buffer consumed by shader/fs-src-buf (samplerBuffer). Verified vs CPU golden: exact count match (14046, 7945, 48167), aggregates match to float precision, visually identical. check.clj asserts the shader structure.

title: Wire FBO generation pass + uniforms into render loop
description: On control change, run a generation FBO pass that writes the splat texture (2*TILE_W x rows) from the field textures via the generation shader, then composite as today. Set per-level uniforms (counts/offsets/scale) + control uniforms. Reuses the existing tiled splat-texture layout.
close_reason: GPU generation via transform feedback: gen.clj VS+GS mirrors seed/splat-record + layer-params + noise; upload-fields! uploads detail/noise/blur/raw/perm textures; TF+geometry-shader compaction captures survivors into a buffer consumed by shader/fs-src-buf (samplerBuffer). Verified vs CPU golden: exact count match (14046, 7945, 48167), aggregates match to float precision, visually identical. check.clj asserts the shader structure.

title: Port splat-record (covariance + colour) to GLSL
description: Mirror seed/splat-record in GLSL: elongation e=1+stroke*coh*(0.25+0.75*dlev), s0 jitter, covariance R(theta) diag Rtranspose, colour blur->raw blend t=0.55+0.45*max(coh,dlev), contrast, tone. check.clj should assert the shader carries this math.
close_reason: GPU generation via transform feedback: gen.clj VS+GS mirrors seed/splat-record + layer-params + noise; upload-fields! uploads detail/noise/blur/raw/perm textures; TF+geometry-shader compaction captures survivors into a buffer consumed by shader/fs-src-buf (samplerBuffer). Verified vs CPU golden: exact count match (14046, 7945, 48167), aggregates match to float precision, visually identical. check.clj asserts the shader structure.

title: Port layered-means placement to GLSL
description: Given a splat index, compute its level, grid position, hash jitter, and (1-detail)-gated Perlin warp -> mean (x,y), size, dlev. CPU precomputes per-level counts/offsets + the budget scale and passes them as uniforms; the shader derives level/position from the global index. Mirrors layered-means. Depends on the GLSL hash/Perlin port.
close_reason: GPU generation via transform feedback: gen.clj VS+GS mirrors seed/splat-record + layer-params + noise; upload-fields! uploads detail/noise/blur/raw/perm textures; TF+geometry-shader compaction captures survivors into a buffer consumed by shader/fs-src-buf (samplerBuffer). Verified vs CPU golden: exact count match (14046, 7945, 48167), aggregates match to float precision, visually identical. check.clj asserts the shader structure.

title: Port hash + Perlin noise to GLSL
description: Reproduce splat-painter.seed/hash01 and splat-painter.noise/noise2 in GLSL so the generation shader can compute the same deterministic jitter and Perlin warp/flow the CPU placement uses. Verify a few values against the CPU fns.
close_reason: GPU generation via transform feedback: gen.clj VS+GS mirrors seed/splat-record + layer-params + noise; upload-fields! uploads detail/noise/blur/raw/perm textures; TF+geometry-shader compaction captures survivors into a buffer consumed by shader/fs-src-buf (samplerBuffer). Verified vs CPU golden: exact count match (14046, 7945, 48167), aggregates match to float precision, visually identical. check.clj asserts the shader structure.

title: Upload precomputed fields as GL textures at load
description: At image load, upload the precomputed fields the generation shader samples: blur (RGB), raw pixels (RGB), flow (theta + coherence), Perlin size/tone (snoise/tnoise), and the wavelet detail map. Use gl-tex-image-2d + RGBA32F. These are the CPU 'sample-fields'/'sample-arr'/'detail-at' sources, moved to texture fetches.
close_reason: GPU generation via transform feedback: gen.clj VS+GS mirrors seed/splat-record + layer-params + noise; upload-fields! uploads detail/noise/blur/raw/perm textures; TF+geometry-shader compaction captures survivors into a buffer consumed by shader/fs-src-buf (samplerBuffer). Verified vs CPU golden: exact count match (14046, 7945, 48167), aggregates match to float precision, visually identical. check.clj asserts the shader structure.
